author: Fishery & Assessment Data Section (FADS) Data Stewardship Unit

description.en: OWL + SKOS ontology for DFO salmon data: stocks/CUs/MUs, survey events, escapement measurements, and GSI constructs; aligns to DwC via rdfs:subClassOf; hybrid approach with SKOS for methods and OWL for events.

comments.en: Migration note (2026-03-15): overlapping shared terms now use `smn:` IRIs directly in this ontology; DFO-specific semantics remain only where no shared replacement exists.

creator.value: Fishery & Assessment Data Section (FADS) Data Stewardship Unit

IAO_0000115.value: Hybrid OWL+SKOS ontology for DFO salmon data. Uses SKOS for controlled vocabularies (methods, criteria) and OWL for formal relationships and events.

publisher.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada (DFO) Pacific Region Science Branch

IAO_0000115.value: An empirically estimated exploitation rate derived from observed or reconstructed catches/removals and returns/escapement for a specified stock and period.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Pacific Salmon - Glossary (Pacific Region). https://www.pac.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/fm-gp/salmon-saumon/gloss-eng.html.

IAO_0000115.value: A fishing mortality rate expressed as the proportion of the total return removed by fishing over a specified assessment period.
The proportion of returning adult salmon removed by fishing activity over a defined period.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Pacific Salmon - Glossary (Pacific Region). https://www.pac.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/fm-gp/salmon-saumon/gloss-eng.html.

IAO_0000115.value: The overall proportion of returning adult salmon removed by all fisheries over a defined period.
The overall proportion of the total return of adult salmon removed by all fisheries over a defined period, aggregating across gear or fishery components.
An existence of a dwc:Organism at a particular place at a particular time.

IAO_0000119.value: https://dwc.tdwg.org/list/

IAO_0000115.value: An occurrent that has temporal parts and for some time t, p s-depends_on some material entity at t.
An action that occurs at some location during some time.

IAO_0000119.value: https://dwc.tdwg.org/list/

IAO_0000115.value: A taxonomic determination (e.g., the assignment to a dwc:Taxon).

IAO_0000119.value: https://dwc.tdwg.org/list/

IAO_0000115.value: The number of mature salmon that pass through (or escape) fisheries and return to fresh water to spawn.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2005. Canada's Policy for Conservation of Wild Pacific Salmon. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/315577.pdf.

IAO_0000115.value: The scientific process of analyzing data to estimate the abundance and productivity of fish stocks in the past, present, and future. It is the backbone of successful science-based fisheries management
The scientific process of analyzing data to estimate abundance and productivity across time.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Introduction to Stock Assessment. https://www.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/science/species-especes/fisheries-halieutiques/assessment-evaluation/module-1/09-eng.html.

IAO_0000115.value: A body of water, usually of smaller size than a lake.
A lentic water body is a water body in which the accumulated water, in its totality, has very little to no directed flow.

prefLabel.value: Wild Salmon Policy biological status zone

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Wild Salmon Policy 2018-2022 Implementation Plan. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40728109.pdf.

definition.value: The biological status of a Conservation Unit (CU) will normally be based on the abundance and distribution of spawners in the unit, or proxies thereof. For each CU, higher and lower benchmarks will be defined that will delimit three status zones: Green, Amber, and Red. As spawner abundance decreases, a CU moves towards the lower status zone, and the extent of management intervention for conservation purposes will increase.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Wild Salmon Policy 2018-2022 Implementation Plan. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40728109.pdf.

definition.value: A Conservation Unit in the Red zone is undesirable because of the risk of extirpation, and the loss of ecological benefits and salmon production. Changes in status will trigger management actions that will vary depending on species, geographic regions, and cause of the decline.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Wild Salmon Policy 2018-2022 Implementation Plan. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40728109.pdf.

definition.value: Social and economic considerations will tend to be the primary drivers for the management of Conservation Units in the Green zone, though ecosystem or other non-consumptive use values could also be considered.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Wild Salmon Policy 2018-2022 Implementation Plan. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40728109.pdf.

definition.value: Amber status implies caution in the management of the Conservation Unit. While a CU in the Amber zone should be at a low risk of loss, there will be a degree of lost production. Decisions about the conservation of CUs in the Amber zone will involve broader consideration of biological, social, and economic issues.

IAO_0000115.value: An entity that can be identified, exists for some period of time, and consists in whole or in part of physical matter while it exists.

IAO_0000119.value: https://dwc.tdwg.org/list/

IAO_0000115.value: An information-defined grouping of fish populations used for reporting, conservation assessment, or management purposes. Examples include Conservation Units (CUs), Stock Management Units (MUs), and Designatable Units (DUs).
An information-defined grouping of fish populations used for reporting, conservation assessment, or management purposes across salmon programs.
A group of organisms of the same species occupying a defined area that interbreed and share a gene pool.

A group of organisms of the same species occupying a defined area that interbreed and share a gene pool. In salmon, populations are composed of one or more demes and form the biological basis for Conservation Units.

comment.en: DFO profile note: this ontology applies `smn:Population` in Wild Salmon Policy / Conservation Unit contexts.

IAO_0000115.value: A local interbreeding group of organisms within a species. In salmon, a deme typically corresponds to a spawning population at a specific site. Demes are the finest biological unit and make up populations.
A local interbreeding group within a species; in salmon systems, typically corresponding to a spawning-site-scale biological unit.

The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) defines a fishery as '...an activity leading to harvesting of fish. It may involve capture of wild fish or raising of fish through aquaculture.'
An independent continuant that is spatially extended whose identity is independent of that of other entities and can be maintained through time.
A dimensionless index or covariate representing marine survival conditions or relative marine survival outcomes (not necessarily a survival proportion).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2021. Marine Survival Forecast of Southern British Columbia Coho (uses climate indicators correlated with marine survival; supports index-like predictors for survival conditions).

IAO_0000115.value: A measurement datum representing an empirically observed rate, count, or abundance derived from monitoring or survey data.
A policy- or management-defined exploitation-rate value used as a target, threshold, or limit reference in decision rules.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Introduction to Stock Assessment - Glossary. https://www.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/science/species-especes/fisheries-halieutiques/assessment-evaluation/glossary-glossaire/index-eng.html.

IAO_0000115.value: The overall proportion of fish in a defined cohort that die over the full assessment interval across all included phases and mortality sources, consistent with the workflow’s accounting (e.g., catch vs escapement vs migration losses where relevant).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2025. Stock-Specific Fraser River Sockeye Salmon Run Size Determination. CSAS Pacific Region Science Advisory Report 2025/021 (run size components include catch, spawning escapement, and en route mortality; other Pacific salmon run sizes typically sum catch + escapement). COSEWIC. 2017. Sockeye Salmon assessment and status report (uses catch, escapement, and en-route loss as distinct accounting components).

IAO_0000115.value: A documented method description linked to how a salmon metric or estimate was produced.
A generically dependent continuant that is about some thing.

The WSP rapid status assessment approach builds on previously completed WSP integrated status assessments. The WSP rapid status approach ensures statuses are scientifically objective, consistent, and comparable across BC/Yukon CUs. This approach is also relatively easy to implement, applicable to data rich and data poor CUs, and can be updated annually.

IAO_0000119.value: Pestal, G., MacDonald, B.L., Grant, S.C.H., and Holt, C.A. 2023. State of the salmon: rapid status assessment approach for Pacific salmon under Canada's Wild Salmon Policy. Canadian Technical Report of Fisheries and Aquatic Sciences 3570.

IAO_0000115.value: A measurement datum is an information content entity that is a recording of the output of a measurement such as produced by a device.
A group of fish sufficiently isolated from other groups that, if extirpated, is very unlikely to recolonize naturally within an acceptable timeframe.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2005. Canada's Policy for Conservation of Wild Pacific Salmon. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/315577.pdf.
Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Canada's Policy for Conservation of Wild Pacific Salmon - Glossary. https://www.pac.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/fm-gp/salmon-saumon/wsp-pss/policy-politique-eng.html#glossary.

IAO_0000115.value: A value specification representing a policy- or model-defined target or limit, such as TAC or UMSY.
A value specification representing a policy- or model-defined target or limit.

label.en: Target or limit rate or abundance

IAO_0000115.value: An operationally defined grouping of salmon used for assessment and management purposes.
An operationally-defined grouping of salmon used for assessment and management purposes. A stock is a management unit or reporting stratum, not a biological organism.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Introduction to Stock Assessment - Glossary. https://www.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/science/species-especes/fisheries-halieutiques/assessment-evaluation/glossary-glossaire/index-eng.html.

IAO_0000115.value: Natural-origin (wild) spawner abundance expressed as a relative (dimensionless) abundance metric to a defined reference (commonly benchmarks), for within-series comparison; where WSP-style relative abundance is used, it is computed using current generational averages (geometric mean) relative to CU-specific benchmarks.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon… CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (relative abundance metric definition). Holt et al. 2009. Indicators of Status and Benchmarks for Conservation Units in Canada's Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Res. Doc. 2009/058 (wild vs hatchery-origin considerations in spawner series context).

IAO_0000115.value: Spawner abundance expressed as a relative (dimensionless) abundance metric to a defined reference (commonly benchmarks), for within-series comparison; in rapid-status workflows, relative abundance is computed using the current generational average (geometric mean) divided by CU-specific lower and/or upper benchmarks.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under Fisheries and Oceans Canada Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (relative abundance metric definition and computation).

IAO_0000115.value: Represents quality annotations, including ratings, quality certificates or feedback that can be associated to datasets or distributions. Quality annotations must have one oa:motivatedBy statement with an instance of oa:Motivation (and skos:Concept) that reflects a quality assessment purpose. We define this instance as dqv:qualityAssessment.

IAO_0000119.value: https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dqv/

IAO_0000115.value: The class for Web Annotations.

IAO_0000119.value: http://www.w3.org/ns/oa#Annotation

IAO_0000115.value: A collection of stocks that are managed together with a joint stock status.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Introduction to Stock Assessment - Glossary. https://www.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/science/species-especes/fisheries-halieutiques/assessment-evaluation/glossary-glossaire/index-eng.html.

IAO_0000115.value: A reporting and management stratum representing a prescribed Pacific fishery management area in Canadian fisheries waters of the Pacific Ocean.

IAO_0000119.value: Government of Canada. Pacific Fishery Management Area Regulations, 2007 (SOR/2007-77): management area means a division of Canadian fisheries waters as enumerated and described in Schedule 2. https://laws-lois.justice.gc.ca/eng/regulations/sor-2007-77/page-1.html.

IAO_0000115.value: A reporting and assessment stratum identifying monitored indicator streams that provide representative indices of spawner abundance for nearby streams.
A river or stream used as a relatively reliable abundance index for nearby salmon returns due to survey intensity and method quality.

comment.en: A salmon-domain observation focused on one feature of interest and one characteristic.

IAO_0000115.value: A field event that collects information about key fish stocks to track their status, and track progress to implement fishery policies and measures for these stocks.
A field event that collects information about fish populations to evaluate status and support management decisions.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. About the Sustainability Survey for Fisheries. https://www.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/reports-rapports/regs/sff-cpd/survey-sondage/about-propos-en.html.

IAO_0000115.value: A survey event specifically for estimating escapement.
A measurement type taken of some physical, biological, or ecological aspect of a fish or fishes.
Benchmark value for a metric used to delineate status categories.
Benchmark value for a metric that helps delineate status zones (for example, the limit reference point for absolute abundance).

IAO_0000119.value: Pestal, G., MacDonald, B.L., Grant, S.C.H., and Holt, C.A. 2023. State of the salmon: rapid status assessment approach for Pacific salmon under Canada's Wild Salmon Policy. Canadian Technical Report of Fisheries and Aquatic Sciences 3570.

comment.en: DFO profile note: this ontology applies `smn:MetricBenchmark` in Wild Salmon Policy status-zone workflows.

IAO_0000115.value: A change metric (delta or percent change, as defined by the workflow) computed from relative natural-origin spawner abundance values over time; in rapid-status workflows, percent change is defined over the most recent three generations using log-transformed abundance series.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon… CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (definition and computation notes for percent change over the most recent three generations).

IAO_0000115.value: A change metric (delta or percent change, as defined by the workflow) computed from relative spawner abundance values over time; where rapid-status percent-change approaches are used, it represents change in abundance over the most recent three generations after log transformation (and smoothing where applicable).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon… CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (definition and computation notes for percent change as a short-term trend over three generations).

IAO_0000115.value: A change metric (delta or percent change, as defined by the workflow) computed from spawner abundance values over time; in rapid-status workflows, percent change is quantified as a linear change in log-transformed abundance over the most recent three generations.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon… CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (percent change definition and computation notes). COSEWIC. 2017. Sockeye Salmon assessment and status report (log-linear rate-of-change model and percent-change derivation over three generations).

IAO_0000115.value: A change metric (delta or percent change, as defined by the workflow) computed from relative hatchery-origin spawner abundance values over time; in rapid-status workflows, percent change is derived from log-transformed series and quantified over the most recent three generations.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon… CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (definition and computation notes for percent change as a short-term trend over three generations).

IAO_0000115.value: Reference points are used as targets to achieve or as limits to avoid.
A target or limit used to guide management actions.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Introduction to Stock Assessment - Glossary. https://www.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/science/species-especes/fisheries-halieutiques/assessment-evaluation/glossary-glossaire/index-eng.html.

comment.en: DFO profile note: this ontology applies `smn:ReferencePoint` in DFO fisheries-management and assessment workflows.

IAO_0000115.value: An assessment artifact describing confidence, uncertainty, and quality constraints for a salmon data product or metric.
The number of salmon which do not get caught by commercial or recreational fisheries and return to their freshwater spawning habitat.
A particular organism or defined group of organisms considered to be taxonomically homogeneous.

IAO_0000119.value: https://dwc.tdwg.org/list/

IAO_0000115.value: A threshold below which fishing must be reduced to avoid reaching the LRP. The USR must be set at an appropriate distance above the LRP to provide sufficient opportunity for managers to recognize a declining stock status and sufficient time for management actions to take effect.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Introduction to Stock Assessment - Glossary. https://www.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/science/species-especes/fisheries-halieutiques/assessment-evaluation/glossary-glossaire/index-eng.html.

IAO_0000115.value: The proportion of fish that die while migrating through the freshwater mainstem portion of a river system (as operationally delimited by the assessment workflow), over a specified interval or between specified mainstem reference points.

IAO_0000119.value: LGL Limited. Migration and Survival of Late-run Fraser Sockeye (radio-telemetry methods; fish detected at Mission are classified as survivors, fishery-related mortalities, or in-river mortalities; fixed-station tracking is conducted at strategic locations including mainstem confluences).

comment.en: A sampling activity that targets a geographic feature, yields a sample, and records a time.

IAO_0000115.value: A collection of information content entities intended to be understood together as a whole.

A trend metric computed from relative hatchery-origin spawner abundance using a defined trend method; in rapid-status workflows, long-term trend is the ratio of current generational average (geometric mean) abundance to long-term average (geometric mean).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon… CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (long-term trend in abundance metric definition).

IAO_0000115.value: A long-term trend metric expressed as the ratio of the current generational average (geometric mean) spawner abundance to the long-term average (geometric mean), yielding a dimensionless measure of relative abundance through time.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon… CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (long-term trend in abundance defined as ratio of current generational average to long-term average).

IAO_0000115.value: A class used to categorize fisheries or survey event instances by operational type.

definition.value: Legacy alias scheme for stock status zones. Use :WSPBiologicalStatusZoneScheme as the canonical scheme for Red/Amber/Green Wild Salmon Policy biological status zones.
Controlled vocabulary for policy and governance frameworks referenced by salmon assessment metrics and benchmarks (e.g., WSP, PA).
Controlled vocabulary for compositional measurement context qualifiers (for example, catch context, run context).
Controlled vocabulary for salmon estimate methods
Controlled vocabulary for salmon enumeration methods
Controlled vocabulary for describing the origin of individual salmon (e.g., natural-origin, hatchery-origin, mixed-origin).
Controlled vocabulary for COSEWIC statuses
Controlled vocabulary for key biological benchmarks, performance indicators, and reference points used in salmon stock assessment and management (e.g., SMSY, Sgen, spawner abundance).
Controlled vocabulary for biological status zones under the Wild Salmon Policy.
Controlled vocabulary for year-basis references used to contextualize salmon measurements and rates (for example, brood year, catch year, return year).
Controlled vocabulary for which age component is represented (total age, freshwater years, or ocean years).
Controlled vocabulary for rapid-status compound variable terms used in WSP rapid-status workflows.
High-level thematic concept scheme for organizing DFO Salmon Ontology classes and concepts.
Controlled vocabulary for estimate type downgrade criteria
Controlled vocabulary for categorical confidence ratings attached to rapid status assessments.
Controlled vocabulary for salmon age notation systems (for example, Gilbert-Rich and European notation).
Controlled vocabulary for integer-like age class values used as constraints on salmon measurements.
Controlled vocabulary for generic benchmark-level qualifiers usable as I-ADOPT constraint values attached to benchmark variables or values.
Controlled vocabulary for Hyatt 1997 estimate types
Controlled vocabulary for the reference event used by an age value (for example, age at return).
Controlled vocabulary for life-phase or fishery/migration segment qualifiers used to stratify measurements (e.g., ocean, terminal, mainstem).

IAO_0000115.value: The proportion of fish that die after reaching terminal spawning areas (or immediate vicinity) but before successfully spawning, as defined by the assessment workflow (i.e., pre-spawn mortality in terminal areas).

IAO_0000119.value: COSEWIC. 2017. Sockeye Salmon (Oncorhynchus nerka) assessment and status report (pre-spawn mortality includes fish that made it to spawning grounds but did not successfully spawn). LGL Limited. Migration and Survival of Late-run Fraser Sockeye (operational identification of pre-spawn mortalities from spawning-area detections).

IAO_0000115.value: Represents criteria relevant for assessing quality. Each quality dimension must have one or more metric to measure it. A dimension is linked with a category using the dqv:inCategory property.

IAO_0000119.value: https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dqv/

definition.value: Phase occurring in terminal areas near river mouths or spawning areas (often including terminal fisheries and local migration to spawning sites).

IAO_0000115.value: The parental year for a group of returning salmon, i.e. the calendar year when the majority of parents of these fish spawned.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Pacific Salmon - Glossary (Pacific Region). https://www.pac.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/fm-gp/salmon-saumon/gloss-eng.html.

definition.value: The parental year for a group of returning salmon, i.e. the calendar year when the majority of parents of these fish spawned.
Incomplete cross-section coverage
Counting/device uptime < 95% (or <90% with interpolation)
Hatchery production, broodstock management, releases, and enhancement program practices for salmon.

IAO_0000115.value: Rapid status confidence category indicating limited supporting data (for example, derived from trend metrics alone).
Rapid status confidence category indicating mixed evidence or reliance on trend metrics supplemented by some abundance information.

definition.value: Model pipeline converts sonar detections to passage totals; sets range/angle/blind zones; species/size attribution rules documented.

comment.en: Legacy methods: Hydroacoustics, Sonar (ARIS/DIDSON). Not Type-1 under Hyatt; interpolation and classification error are central downgrades.

IAO_0000115.value: A numeric score produced by a rapid-status algorithm used to represent an ordered status outcome and/or decision-rule position.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under DFO’s Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004.

definition.value: A numeric score produced by a rapid-status algorithm used to represent an ordered status outcome and/or decision-rule position.
Salmon age notation that records freshwater years followed by ocean years (for example, 1.2).
Measurement context indicating values represent survival, mortality, or related rates.
Redd counts with documentation of detectability, timing, and reach coverage. Conversion to spawner estimates requires spawners-per-redd factors handled in analysis.
An upper-threshold benchmark value, typically delineating the boundary between Amber and Green status zones.
Detectability factors not validated
Uses the peak survey (live ± dead variants) as the season estimate/index.

comment.en: Legacy methods: Peak Live, Peak Live + Dead, Peak + Cumulative Dead, Cumulative New

definition.value: Precision/accuracy weaker than expected for type
Poor visibility conditions

Two or more trained snorkelers conduct standardized passes within defined segments, tallying live/dead by species. Field forms capture visibility class, segment boundaries, and repeat-visit schedule; counts are aggregated across observers/passes.
Interpolation used for missing data; defensibility must be documented

IAO_0000115.value: Rapid status confidence category indicating that high-quality abundance metrics support the result.

definition.value: Age class value 3 years.
Age value represents total age in years.
Criteria that may result in downgrading an escapement estimate type classification
Visual surveys conducted by stream/bank walk with documented visit schedule, reach coverage, timing, and visibility conditions.

comment.en: Legacy methods: Stream Walk, Ground count, Bank Walk

definition.value: Measurement context indicating categorical or ordinal quality ratings.
Age class value 2 years.
A policy, legal, or governance framework that defines how salmon status metrics, benchmarks, or reference points are set and interpreted.
Field observations, sampling protocols, survey events, gear, and measurement methods used to collect salmon data.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Wild Salmon Policy 2018-2022 Implementation Plan. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40728109.pdf.

definition.value: Social and economic considerations will tend to be the primary drivers for the management of Conservation Units in the Green zone, though ecosystem or other non-consumptive use values could also be considered.
Insufficient number of survey visits
Origin category describing whether salmon are naturally produced, hatchery produced, or mixed-origin.
Genetic markers, assays, and analyses used to infer stock composition, parentage, and population structure.
Measurement context indicating values are management benchmarks or reference-oriented metrics.
Effort not standardized
Age measured at return migration (adult return timing).
DFO policy framework for applying precaution in fisheries decision-making, including use of reference points and stock status zones.
Age class value 7 years.
Regression/state-space model calibrated to a Type-1/2 series converts a lower-precision index to abundance.

comment.en: Document calibration source years, transferability, diagnostics. Type-2 when calibration robust and applicable; else Type-3.

definition.value: A phase or segment used to stratify salmon measurements by where or when they apply (e.g., ocean phase, terminal phase).
Overflights (helicopter, fixed wing, or drone) with documented flight lines, coverage, visibility, and sensor settings. Imagery may be reviewed post-flight.

comment.en: Legacy methods: Helicopter, Fixed Wing Aircraft, Aerial, Drone, UAS, UAV

IAO_0000115.value: A long-term trend metric comparing the current generational average (geometric mean) spawner abundance to the long-term average (geometric mean) spawner abundance for an assessed time series.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under DFO’s Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004.

definition.value: A long-term trend metric comparing the current generational average (geometric mean) spawner abundance to the long-term average (geometric mean) spawner abundance for an assessed time series.
Environmental conditions outside specifications

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2018. Review of genetically based targets for enhanced contributions to Canadian Pacific Chinook Salmon populations. DFO Canadian Science Advisory Secretariat Science Advisory Report 2018/001 (Erratum: October 2023).

definition.value: Individuals that are born and reared in the wild (natural environment).
Integrates serial counts over time using a survey-life parameter; may apply observer efficiency.

Non-numeric, year-specific record of presence/not detected (i.e., present/absent without estimating a count), tied to an explicit time and location, with reliable species identification. Underlying field methods may be highly variable; analysis is not required.
Legal provisions in Canada's Fisheries Act and regulations related to maintaining and rebuilding fish stocks, including obligations triggered when reference points are breached.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Wild Salmon Policy 2018-2022 Implementation Plan. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40728109.pdf.

definition.value: Amber status implies caution in the management of the Conservation Unit. While a CU in the Amber zone should be at a low risk of loss, there will be a degree of lost production. Decisions about the conservation of CUs in the Amber zone will involve broader consideration of biological, social, and economic issues.
Trap efficiency not measured/validated
Freshwater in-river phase (migration and/or residence) outside the marine environment.

Sonar detections (ARIS, DIDSON, or other hydroacoustic systems) with documented coverage, visibility/noise conditions, and uptime. Raw detections require processing through a modelling/classification pipeline.
Insufficient reach coverage

IAO_0000115.value: A year-based temporal reference used to contextualize measurements or rates (e.g., brood year, catch year, or return year).

definition.value: A year-based temporal reference used to contextualize measurements or rates (e.g., brood year, catch year, or return year).
Measurement context indicating values refer to run size or returning run composition.
Trap-based counts adjusted with efficiency/coverage models to estimate total passage.

comment.en: Requires documented trap efficiency measurements and cross-section coverage factors.

definition.value: Capture-recapture estimators (Petersen, Jolly-Seber, open/Bayesian models) applied to field marks/recoveries.

comment.en: Legacy methods: Petersen, Jolly-Seber, Open model, Bayesian MR. Store uncertainty (e.g., CV) when available; note tag loss/recovery protocols.

definition.value: Classification/attribution not documented
Age class value 5 years.
A method used to analyze and estimate salmon abundance from enumeration data
Data models, analytical workflows, provenance, quality dimensions, and metadata supporting salmon science.
Age measured at the time of sampling or observation.
Habitat attributes, ecosystem conditions, and climate or environmental stressors affecting salmon.
An integer-like age class value used as a compositional constraint in salmon measurements.
Age value represents years spent in the marine environment.
Conservation Unit based stock assessment methods, metrics, and metadata

IAO_0000115.value: The calendar year in which adult salmon return from the ocean to freshwater systems (a year basis used to contextualize run/return-related measurements).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Pacific Salmon Glossary (Pacific Region web page).

definition.value: The calendar year in which adult salmon return from the ocean to freshwater systems (a year basis used to contextualize run/return-related measurements).

IAO_0000115.value: A data quality category indicating that no spawner-abundance estimate classification / index quality rating was assigned for the record (i.e., an 'Unknown' estimate classification with no quality rating).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2020. 2020 summary of abundance data for Chinook salmon (Oncorhynchus tshawytscha) in southern British Columbia, Canada (nuSEDS Estimate Classification includes 'Unknown: no quality rating').

definition.value: A data quality category indicating that no spawner-abundance estimate classification / index quality rating was assigned for the record (i.e., an 'Unknown' estimate classification with no quality rating).
Device not within operational specifications
Poor timing of surveys
DFO policy frameworks, governance, regulations, and organizational structure relevant to salmon management.
Total, seasonal counts through fence or fishway; virtually no bypass. Simple, often single step analysis. Reliable resolution of between year differences >10% (in absolute units).

Relative abundance index (i.e., not an absolute count) derived from moderate to high effort surveys (typically 3 or more site visits) using standardized methods applied consistently between and within years (e.g., equal-effort surveys executed by walk, swim, overflight). Accuracy and precision are not fully quantified (i.e., no explicit variance estimate), but are assumed stable enough for within-series comparisons. Reliable resolution of between-year differences >25% (in relative units).

Indirect estimate of total spawner abundance with qualified accuracy and known precision (i.e., a variance estimate—an explicit quantitative measure of uncertainty around the estimate). Derived from high-effort, standardized methods (e.g., mark-recapture, serial counts for area under the curve) with documented analytical conversions (including interpolation/calibration as needed). Reported in absolute units (estimate ± variance). Reliable resolution of between-year differences >25% (in absolute units).
Measurement context indicating values refer to spawners or spawning-stage fish.

IAO_0000115.value: A probability metric estimating the risk that spawner abundance will be below the lower benchmark, given uncertainty in benchmark estimates and/or abundance estimates, for a defined forecast or assessment horizon.

prefLabel.value: Probability of decline below lower benchmark metric

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under DFO’s Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004.

definition.value: A probability metric estimating the risk that spawner abundance will be below the lower benchmark, given uncertainty in benchmark estimates and/or abundance estimates, for a defined forecast or assessment horizon.
Insufficient QA review of automated events

Low effort (e.g. 1 trip), use of vaguely defined, inconsistent or poorly executed methods. Unknown to ill defined; inconsistent or poorly executed. Uncertain numeric comparisons, but high reliability for presence or absence.
Low to moderate effort (1-4 trips), known survey method. Simple analysis by known methods. Reliable resolution of between year differences >200% (in relative units).

IAO_0000115.value: A multiplicative factor used to expand observed counts or detections from a sampled subset (e.g., indicator sites, PIT detections) to an estimated total abundance or escapement.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2021. Marine Survival Forecast of Southern British Columbia Coho (describes applying expansion factors to PIT detections to estimate total escapement).

definition.value: A multiplicative factor used to expand observed counts or detections from a sampled subset (e.g., indicator sites, PIT detections) to an estimated total abundance or escapement.

Salmon are guided through a controlled opening in a fence/weir where trained observers visually tally each passage event on a continuous or daily schedule. Counting chambers/windows and SIL/SEN daily logs document coverage; bypass routes are monitored and closed where possible.

comment.en: Legacy methods: Fence, Weir, Fixed Site Census (manual)

definition.value: Measurement context indicating values refer to recruits.
Survey/analytical methods not adequately identified/consistent
Classification of escapement estimate quality based on Hyatt 1997 framework
Bypass or breach risks not monitored and verified negligible
Canada's Wild Salmon Policy framework for conserving and managing wild Pacific salmon, including integrated status assessments and biological benchmarks for Conservation Units.
Age measured at maturity.

IAO_0000115.value: The calendar year in which returning salmon are caught in fisheries (as opposed to the brood year in which the cohort was spawned).

definition.value: The calendar year in which returning salmon are caught in fisheries (as opposed to the brood year in which the cohort was spawned).
Missing SIL/SEN logs or QA/methods report
Effort-standardized electrofishing passes used to generate catch-per-unit-effort (CPUE) indices.

IAO_0000119.value: Pestal, G., MacDonald, B.L., Grant, S.C.H., and Holt, C.A. 2023. State of the salmon: rapid status assessment approach for Pacific salmon under Canada's Wild Salmon Policy. Canadian Technical Report of Fisheries and Aquatic Sciences 3570.

definition.value: The analytical method used to produce rapid-status metrics under the Wild Salmon Policy rapid-status approach.
A compound variable concept representing a metric used by WSP rapid-status workflows.
Age class value 4 years.
Run-window coverage < 95% of season days
Converts redd counts to spawner estimates using spawners-per-redd expansion factors.

comment.en: Requires validated spawners-per-redd factor and documentation of detectability assumptions.

IAO_0000115.value: The generational average of spawner abundance, calculated as a geometric mean across the number of years corresponding to the most common age at return (generation length), optionally after smoothing depending on CU group.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under DFO’s Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004.

definition.value: The generational average of spawner abundance, calculated as a geometric mean across the number of years corresponding to the most common age at return (generation length), optionally after smoothing depending on CU group.
Field program involving marking and subsequent recapture/recovery of tagged fish with documented assumptions and recovery coverage.

IAO_0000115.value: An abundance percentile metric giving the percentile rank of the current generational average spawner abundance relative to a historical reference distribution.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under DFO’s Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004.

definition.value: An abundance percentile metric giving the percentile rank of the current generational average spawner abundance relative to a historical reference distribution.

IAO_0000115.value: A categorical attribute indicating whether an abundance time series is recorded as absolute abundance or as a relative index (used by rapid-status workflows).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under DFO’s Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004.

definition.value: A categorical attribute indicating whether an abundance time series is recorded as absolute abundance or as a relative index (used by rapid-status workflows).

scopeNote.value: In SPSR rapid-status workflows, this field distinguishes absolute abundance vs relative index inputs (often abbreviated in code as 'Abs_Abd' and 'RelIdx').

definition.value: Measurement context indicating values refer to fishery catch.
MR assumptions not evaluated
Salmon age notation where total age is annotated with freshwater years as a subscript-like suffix (for example, 4_2 for total age 4 with 2 freshwater years).
Fishery openings, harvest control rules, catch/effort measures, and exploitation monitoring for salmon fisheries.
Age class value 1 year.
Trap-based counts from non-spanning traps with documented emptying schedule, efficiency measurements, and coverage information.

comment.en: Legacy methods: Trap, Hatchery Passage Counting

definition.value: Phase of the salmon life cycle or assessment period occurring in the marine environment.
Seasonal total = roll‑up of daily/continuous counts from a fixed (non‑sonar) station; simple arithmetic with documented coverage.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Wild Salmon Policy 2018-2022 Implementation Plan. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40728109.pdf.

definition.value: A Conservation Unit in the Red zone is undesirable because of the risk of extirpation, and the loss of ecological benefits and salmon production. Changes in status will trigger management actions that will vary depending on species, geographic regions, and cause of the decline.
Age class value 6 years.
A notation system used to encode salmon age values in stock assessment data.
COSEWIC/SARA recovery planning, critical habitat, threats, and status assessments for species of conservation concern.
Age value represents years spent in freshwater.
A context qualifier used to disambiguate the interpretation of a measurement column.
A context qualifier used to disambiguate interpretation of a measurement.
The age component represented by a value (total age, freshwater years, or ocean years).
Stock assessment models, benchmarks, status determinations, and performance metrics for salmon units.
A lower-threshold benchmark value, typically delineating the boundary between Red and Amber status zones.
Applies expansion factors or arithmetic transforms to a base count/index.

comment.en: Legacy methods: Lake Expansion, Redd Expansion, Peak×Factor, (Peak+CumDead)×Factor, Addition/Subtraction, Multiplication/Division. Inherits base method's Type; weak provenance = downgrade one Type.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2018. Review of genetically based targets for enhanced contributions to Canadian Pacific Chinook Salmon populations. DFO Canadian Science Advisory Secretariat Science Advisory Report 2018/001 (Erratum: October 2023).

definition.value: Individuals that were born or reared in a hatchery facility.
Freshwater mainstem migration phase in a river system (often corresponding to mainstem in-river fisheries or monitoring segments).

IAO_0000115.value: A benchmark-ratio metric equal to the ratio of current generational average spawner abundance to a specified benchmark (lower or upper benchmark), where 1.0 indicates equality to that benchmark.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under DFO’s Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004.

definition.value: A benchmark-ratio metric equal to the ratio of current generational average spawner abundance to a specified benchmark (lower or upper benchmark), where 1.0 indicates equality to that benchmark.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under DFO’s Wild Salmon Policy. CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004.

IAO_0000115.value: A short-term trend metric quantifying the percent (linear) change in spawner abundance over the most recent three generations.

definition.value: A short-term trend metric quantifying the percent (linear) change in spawner abundance over the most recent three generations.
The reference event or life-history point that an age value is measured against.

Automated counting at a constrained opening using electronic/optical counters, resistivity counters, or video/camera systems. QA review of automated events, cross-section coverage, and uptime/coverage are documented.

comment.en: Legacy methods: Electronic/Optical counter, Resistivity Counter, Video/Camera counter

IAO_0000115.value: A small, slow-moving stream or creek; usually located in low-lying areas.
A watercourse which is linear and flows across the solid portion of a planetary surface.

comment.en: A creek is typically smaller than a stream.

IAO_0000115.value: The percentile rank of relative hatchery-origin spawner abundance against a defined historical reference distribution.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations… CSAS Science Response 2024/004 (percentile benchmarks discussion).

IAO_0000115.value: The percentile rank of relative spawner abundance against a defined historical reference distribution.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations… CSAS Science Response 2024/004 (percentile benchmarks discussion).

IAO_0000115.value: The percentile rank of relative natural-origin spawner abundance against a defined historical reference distribution.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations… CSAS Science Response 2024/004 (percentile benchmarks discussion).

IAO_0000115.value: The percentile rank of a spawner abundance value (annual or period-specific) against a defined historical reference distribution.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations… CSAS Science Response 2024/004 (percentile benchmarks discussion).

IAO_0000115.value: Hatchery-origin spawner abundance expressed as a relative (dimensionless) abundance metric to a defined reference (commonly benchmarks), for within-series comparison; in rapid-status workflows, relative abundance is computed using the current generational average (geometric mean) divided by CU-specific lower and/or upper benchmarks.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon… CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (relative abundance metric definition and computation). Holt et al. 2009. Indicators of Status and Benchmarks… CSAS Res. Doc. 2009/058 (discusses hatchery/wild considerations for status assessments).

IAO_0000115.value: A continuant that is dependent on one or more independent continuants and whose instances can be multiply instantiated.
year of measurement
A lotic water body is a water body in which the accumulated water, in its totality, is flowing.

A lower fisheries-management reference point defined for Stock Management Units (SMUs) and related fishery decision frameworks. It may be informed by Conservation Unit (CU) lower biological benchmarks, but is not identical to CU-level lower biological benchmarks used to delineate Wild Salmon Policy biological status zones.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. (2022). Methodologies and Guidelines for Defining Limit Reference Points for Pacific Salmon. DFO Can. Sci. Advis. Sec. Sci. Advis. Rep. 2022/030. (Erratum: December 2022).

IAO_0000115.value: A fish stock experiencing serious harm may be depleted to the point where it has impaired productivity or reproductive capacity. As a result, the stock would be less resilient to fishing and could not easily rebuild. Serious harm may be irreversible, or only slowly reversible over the long-term, and can be due to fishing, other human-induced impacts, or natural causes. Under the Precautionary Approach policy and the Fish Stocks Provisions (FSPs), breaching the LRP is a trigger for a rebuilding plan.
A lower threshold indicating stock status where stronger mitigation or rebuilding action is triggered.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Introduction to Stock Assessment - Glossary. https://www.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/science/species-especes/fisheries-halieutiques/assessment-evaluation/glossary-glossaire/index-eng.html.

IAO_0000115.value: Group of individuals of a species which can be regarded as an entity for management or assessment purposes; a separate breeding population of a species; term used to identify a management unit of fishery species. A distinct genetic population, a population defined by movement pattern, part of a population potentially harvestable, or a quantity of fish from a given area. May be a total or a spawning stock.
The abundance (count) of adult salmon that reach spawning grounds in a given system and time period (often operationalized as escapement/spawning escapement).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Pacific Salmon Glossary (defines escapement/spawning escapement as the number of salmon returning to the spawning grounds).

IAO_0000115.value: A measurement or estimate of escapement tied to a stock and a survey event.
A trend metric computed from spawner abundance over time using a defined trend method (e.g., slope over a specified window).

IAO_0000119.value: Derived for SPSR term coverage; distinct from gcdfo:LongTermTrendMetric (current generational average vs long-term average) in CSAS Science Response 2024/004.

IAO_0000115.value: A body of water or other liquid of considerable size contained in a depression on a landmass.

A stream which, through permanent or seasonal flow processes, moves from elevated land towards lower elevations through a definite channel and empties either into a sea, lake, or another river or ends on land as bed seepage and evapotranspiration exceed water supply.

comment.en: Crosswalk used MaterialEntity; aligned here to Darwin Core MaterialSample for concrete publication workflows.

IAO_0000115.value: Lower benchmark value for a Conservation Unit (CU) metric used to delineate Wild Salmon Policy status zones (typically the boundary between Red and Amber zones). This CU-level biological benchmark is distinct from SMU-level fisheries management lower reference points such as FRP-L.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. (2021). Wild Salmon Policy 2018-2022 Implementation Plan. Available at https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40728109.pdf.

IAO_0000115.value: The proportion of fish in a defined population/cohort that die during freshwater migration in-river on the way to spawning areas during a specified assessment interval, excluding landed catch (fishery removals) where those are accounted separately.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2025. Stock-Specific Fraser River Sockeye Salmon Run Size Determination. CSAS Pacific Region Science Advisory Report 2025/021 (en route mortality excluding landed catch). COSEWIC. 2017. Sockeye Salmon (Oncorhynchus nerka) assessment and status report (en-route loss is fish that perished in the river en-route to spawning grounds for reasons other than fishing).

IAO_0000115.value: A directive information entity with action specifications and objective specifications as parts, and that may be concretized as a realizable entity that, if realized, is realized in a process in which the bearer tries to achieve the objectives by taking the actions specified.

definition.value: A method used to enumerate or count salmon in the field

scopeNote.value: DFO profile note: this ontology uses the shared `smn:EnumerationMethod` concept directly and constrains local narrower methods beneath it.

definition.value: Categorical confidence rating (High, Medium, Low) for stock assessment metrics.

IAO_0000115.value: Rapid status confidence category indicating limited supporting data (for example, derived from trend metrics alone).
Rapid status confidence category indicating mixed evidence or reliance on trend metrics supplemented by some abundance information.
Rapid status confidence category indicating that high-quality abundance metrics support the result.

The proportion of fish that die during the ocean (marine) life-history phase for a defined population/cohort and interval; when a marine survival proportion is available, ocean mortality may be represented as its complement under the assessment’s accounting conventions.

IAO_0000119.value: COSEWIC. 2017. Sockeye Salmon (Oncorhynchus nerka) assessment and status report (marine survival index described and interpreted in a Pacific salmon assessment context).

IAO_0000115.value: The proportion of spawners in a population-year that are hatchery-origin (hatchery-origin spawners divided by total spawners).

IAO_0000119.value: Derived for SPSR term coverage (Issue #33 term request scope); ‘hatchery-origin’ is defined in the Salmon Origin scheme and operational marking is described in the DFO Coded Wire Tag Program page.

IAO_0000115.value: A marine survival proportion estimated for a coded-wire-tag (CWT) release group, defined as the portion of tagged smolts that survive to be caught in marine fisheries or return to freshwater as adults.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2021. Marine Survival Forecast of Southern British Columbia Coho (defines marine survival as (Catch+Escapement)/Release for coded-wire-tag smolt releases).

IAO_0000115.value: A trend metric computed from relative natural-origin spawner abundance using a defined trend method; in rapid-status workflows, long-term trend is the ratio of current generational average (geometric mean) abundance to long-term average (geometric mean).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon… CSAS Pacific Region Science Response 2024/004 (long-term trend in abundance metric definition).

IAO_0000115.value: b is a specifically dependent continuant = Def. b is a continuant & there is some independent continuant c which is not a spatial region and which is such that b s-depends_on c at every time t during the course of b’s existence. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [050-003])
An information content entity whose concretizations indicate to their bearer how to realize them in a process.
Upper benchmark value for a metric used to delineate Wild Salmon Policy status zones (typically the boundary between Amber and Green zones).

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. Wild Salmon Policy 2018-2022 Implementation Plan. https://waves-vagues.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/library-bibliotheque/40728109.pdf.

IAO_0000115.value: Threshold value used by the rapid-status algorithm that may differ from expert-defined benchmarks to incorporate precautionary buffers.

IAO_0000119.value: Fisheries and Oceans Canada. 2024. Rapid Status Approximations for Pacific Salmon Derived from Integrated Expert Assessments under Fisheries and Oceans Canada Wild Salmon Policy. DFO Canadian Science Advisory Secretariat Science Response 2024/004.

IAO_0000115.value: Relates a material sample to the reporting or management stratum it is taken to represent in analysis.
Relates a population to one of its constituent demes.
Relates a stock-assessment process to the reporting/management stratum or biological population it evaluates.
Relates a reporting/management stratum or biological population to a stock assessment that evaluates it.
Relates a Conservation Unit to one of its constituent populations.
Relates a reporting or management stratum to one of its constituent populations.

comment.en: DFO profile note: within this ontology, `smn:hasPopulation` is primarily used for ConservationUnit-to-Population composition.

IAO_0000115.value: Relates a target, threshold, or limit exploitation-rate value to the observed exploitation-rate estimate it evaluates.

label.en: is target or limit for observed exploitation rate

IAO_0000115.value: Relates a measurement datum to a stock assessment process that uses it as evidence.
Relates a stock assessment to a measurement datum used as assessment input or evidence.

comment.en: Range explicitly includes measurement datum plus common rate classes used in stock-assessment workflows.

IAO_0000115.value: Relates a Conservation Unit to the Stock Management Unit it belongs to.
Relates a Stock Management Unit to one of its constituent Conservation Units.
Represents the dimensions a quality metric, certificate and annotation allow a measurement of.

IAO_0000119.value: https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dqv/

comment.en: Dimensions are meant to systematically organize metrics, quality certificates and quality annotations. The Data Quality Vocabulary defines no specific cardinality constraints for dqv:inDimension, since distinct quality frameworks might have different perspectives over a metric. A metric may therefore be associated to more than one dimension. However, those who define new quality metrics should try to avoid this as much as possible and assign only one dimension to the metrics they define. More than one dimension can be indicated for each quality annotation or certificate.

IAO_0000115.value: Relates a survey event to the reporting or management stratum that the event targets for inference or status interpretation.
Links a measurement or rate to the year basis (e.g., brood year, catch year, or return year) it is reported against.

comment.en: Expected values are concepts in :YearBasisScheme (for example, gcdfo:BroodYear, gcdfo:CatchYear, and gcdfo:ReturnYear).

IAO_0000115.value: Relates a deme to the population it belongs to.
Relates a survey event to a measurement datum/result it produces for salmon assessment workflows.
Relates a population to the reporting or management stratum it belongs to.
Relates a population to the Conservation Unit it belongs to.

comment.en: DFO profile note: within this ontology, `smn:populationOf` is primarily used for Population-to-ConservationUnit composition.

IAO_0000115.value: Relates a survey event to the procedure/protocol used to execute it.
Relates a stock assessment process to a reference point used to interpret status or management risk.
Relates a reference point to a stock assessment process that uses it for status interpretation or advice.
Relates a survey event to a variable compound term represented as a SKOS concept that defines what was measured or derived in SDP workflows.
Annotation used on a variable SKOS concept to identify the procedure, protocol, or computational method used to derive the variable.
Relates an observed exploitation-rate estimate to the target, threshold, or limit exploitation-rate value used to evaluate it.
